# Resource (hush_twilio_access_credential)

Manage Twilio dynamic access credentials in the Hush Security platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create a Twilio dynamic access credential
resource "hush_twilio_access_credential" "example" {
  name           = "prod-twilio"
  description    = "Production Twilio credential"
  deployment_ids = [hush_deployment.example.id]
  account_sid    = "ACxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
  api_key_sid    = var.twilio_api_key_sid

  api_key_secret_wo         = var.twilio_api_key_secret
  api_key_secret_wo_version = 1
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `account_sid` (String) The Twilio Account SID
- `api_key_sid` (String) The Twilio API Key SID
- `deployment_ids` (List of String) List of deployment IDs that can access this credential
- `name` (String) The name of the Twilio access credential

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `api_key_secret` (String, Sensitive) The Twilio API Key Secret
- `api_key_secret_wo` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The Twilio API Key Secret (write-only). More secure than `api_key_secret` because Terraform will not store this value in the state file. Either `api_key_secret` or `api_key_secret_wo` must be specified.
- `api_key_secret_wo_version` (String) Used to trigger updates for `api_key_secret_wo`. Change when the secret changes.
- `description` (String) The description of the Twilio access credential

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the Twilio access credential
- `kind` (String) The kind of access credential
- `type` (String) The type of access credential
